The period after the Sun is half-sec and before the stars have
appeared is known as Sandhya (evening twilight), and that after the
stars have lost their lustre and before the Sun is half-risen is the
morning twilight. From the above-mentioned symptoms of the two
twilights (or junctures) one should predict good or bad effects. If-
all the features are glossy, there will be rain the same day; if rough,
there will be danger.
[Vide XXX. I]
